what_it_does: Defines a reusable React tweaks panel for edit-mode prototypes, including injected CSS, a tweak state hook, host postMessage protocol handling, draggable floating panel behavior, and form controls for sliders, toggles, radios, selects, text, numbers, colors, and buttons.
observations: The file is self-contained and exports all public helpers by assigning them to window.
The usage header documents the intended edit-mode defaults block and control patterns.
The panel announces edit-mode availability to the parent window and responds to activate/deactivate messages.
Tweak updates are stored in React state, posted to the parent host, and dispatched locally as a tweakchange event.
Styling is embedded as a template string and injected only while the panel is open.
The radio control switches to a select when option labels are too long or there are more than three options.
Color controls support curated single-color or palette chips, with a native color input fallback.
standouts: Combines edit-mode host protocol, persistence signaling, and UI controls in one file-local helper layer.
Provides curated palette selection with checkmark contrast calculation.
Includes drag handling for both the panel shell and segmented radio control selection.
Uses type-preserving option resolution for TweakRadio's select fallback.
conventions: Uses global React and browser APIs rather than imports or module exports.
Uses function declarations for exported components and local arrow functions for handlers/helpers.
Keeps control markup class-based while defining all styles in a single CSS string.
Uses inline comments to explain protocol timing, fallback thresholds, and type-preservation behavior.
Uses raw CSS values, hex colors, and pixel dimensions under a ds-adherence ignore header.
concerns: The host messages use wildcard postMessage targets.
The panel drag path uses mouse events, while some controls use pointer events.
TweakSelect emits string values directly, so callers receive strings unless a wrapper such as TweakRadio's resolve path maps them back.
Window-level drag and pointer listeners are attached imperatively and rely on matching completion events for cleanup.
notes: This file is the edit-mode tweaks shell plus a compact control library for prototypes that persist tweak values through a parent host.
questions: Should same-window tweakchange be dispatched for host-originated changes if such changes exist?
Should the panel drag interaction support touch and pen input like the pointer-based controls?
Is wildcard postMessage intentional for every embedding context this helper targets?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it owns the host edit-mode protocol and provides a reusable tweaks shell with consistent form controls.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to let individual prototypes opt into a consistent edit-mode panel without reimplementing host messaging, persistence updates, or common tweak controls.
novelty: The file combines a parent-frame edit-mode protocol with an in-page React control kit and curated palette chips in a single globally exported helper.
contribution: Contributes a self-contained tweak authoring surface: host activation wiring, state-to-host persistence signaling, draggable panel chrome, and reusable controls for numeric, boolean, categorical, text, and color design parameters.
summary_for_embedding: A self-contained React tweaks-panel helper that injects styling, manages edit-mode host messaging, persists tweak edits, and exposes reusable UI controls on window.
intention_for_embedding: Built to give prototypes a consistent edit-mode customization panel whose changes can be written back by a parent host.

symbols.does: Creates tweak state and a setter that updates local state, notifies the parent host, and dispatches a local event.
symbols.description: The hook initializes React state from defaults and returns the current values plus setTweak. setTweak accepts either a key/value pair or an object of edits, merges edits into state, posts __edit_mode_set_keys to the parent, and emits a same-window tweakchange event.

symbols.does: Renders the floating tweaks panel and manages edit-mode visibility, dragging, viewport clamping, and dismissal.
symbols.description: The component listens for host activation messages, announces availability, injects the panel CSS while open, and renders the panel header/body shell. It tracks right/bottom offsets for dragging and clamps the panel inside the viewport on open, resize, and document resize observation.

symbols.does: Handles parent window edit-mode activation and deactivation messages.
symbols.description: This message handler reads e.data.type and toggles the TweaksPanel open state when it sees __activate_edit_mode or __deactivate_edit_mode. It is installed before the component posts its availability message.

symbols.does: Locally closes the panel and tells the parent host that edit mode was dismissed.
symbols.description: The function sets open false and posts __edit_mode_dismissed to window.parent. The surrounding comment states that the host echoes deactivation back to keep toolbar state synchronized.

symbols.does: Starts mouse-based dragging for the floating tweaks panel.
symbols.description: The handler captures the panel's initial rectangle, pointer coordinates, and right/bottom offsets. It installs window mousemove and mouseup listeners that update the stored offsets and remove themselves when the drag ends.

symbols.does: Updates panel right/bottom offsets during panel dragging.
symbols.description: This drag move handler computes offset deltas from the original mouse position and stores the new right and bottom values. It then calls clampToViewport so the panel remains within the visible window padding.

symbols.does: Ends panel dragging and removes mouse listeners.
symbols.description: This mouseup handler unregisters the window mousemove and mouseup listeners that were attached by onDragStart. It performs cleanup for the panel drag interaction.

symbols.does: Renders a section label followed by optional child controls.
symbols.description: The component emits a fragment containing a .twk-sect label div and any children passed to it. It is a simple layout helper for grouping controls inside the panel.

symbols.does: Wraps a labeled control row with optional value display and inline layout.
symbols.description: The component renders a row with a label, an optional right-side value readout, and child content. The inline prop switches between stacked and horizontal row classes.

symbols.does: Renders a labeled range input that emits numeric values.
symbols.description: The component displays the current value with an optional unit in a TweakRow. Its range input forwards min, max, and step props and calls onChange with Number(e.target.value).

symbols.does: Renders a boolean switch button.
symbols.description: The component lays out a label and switch-styled button with role switch and aria-checked. Clicking it sends the negated boolean value through onChange.

symbols.does: Renders a segmented radio control or falls back to TweakSelect for long or numerous options.
symbols.description: The component normalizes options, measures label lengths, and decides whether they fit in the segmented UI. For fitted options it renders a draggable segmented control with an animated thumb; otherwise it delegates to TweakSelect with a resolver that maps selected strings back to original option values.

symbols.does: Returns the display-label string length for a radio option.
symbols.description: The helper handles both primitive options and object options with label fields. TweakRadio uses it to decide whether options fit as segmented buttons.

symbols.does: Maps a select string value back to the matching original radio option value.
symbols.description: This helper searches the options array by stringified value. It preserves object option values when a TweakRadio fallback uses a native select whose event value is always a string.

symbols.does: Calculates which segmented radio option corresponds to a clientX position.
symbols.description: The helper reads the segment track bounds, accounts for the track padding, converts the pointer position into an option index, and clamps that index to the available options. It returns the selected option value.

symbols.does: Starts segmented radio selection by pointer position and installs drag listeners.
symbols.description: The handler marks the segmented control as dragging, selects the initial segment, and attaches window pointermove and pointerup listeners. It uses valueRef so drag movement compares against the current active value rather than a stale closure.

symbols.does: Updates segmented radio selection while dragging across the track.
symbols.description: This pointermove handler checks that the track still exists, computes the option under the pointer with segAt, and calls onChange only when the value differs from the current ref. It is scoped to TweakRadio's drag interaction.

symbols.does: Ends segmented radio dragging and removes pointer listeners.
symbols.description: This pointerup handler clears the dragging state and unregisters the pointermove and pointerup listeners installed by onPointerDown. It completes the segmented control drag lifecycle.

symbols.does: Renders a labeled select control from primitive or object options.
symbols.description: The component wraps a native select in TweakRow, deriving each option's value and label from either an object or primitive. Its onChange passes the native select event value string to the caller.

symbols.does: Renders a labeled text input.
symbols.description: The component wraps an input type text in TweakRow. It accepts value and placeholder props and forwards typed text through onChange.

symbols.does: Renders a compact numeric input with optional unit and horizontal scrubbing on the label.
symbols.description: The component provides numeric clamping, a number input, and pointer-based scrubbing from the label. Scrub movement increments by step, rounds to the step precision, clamps to min/max, and emits the resulting number.

symbols.does: Constrains a numeric value to optional min and max bounds.
symbols.description: The helper returns min when the input is below min, max when it is above max, and otherwise the original number. It is used by TweakNumber for both typed and scrubbed values.

symbols.does: Starts label-based numeric scrubbing for TweakNumber.
symbols.description: The handler prevents default selection behavior, records the start pointer position and value, computes step decimal precision, and attaches pointermove and pointerup listeners. Its move listener converts horizontal motion into stepped numeric changes.

symbols.does: Updates a numeric value during label scrubbing.
symbols.description: This pointermove handler computes horizontal delta from the scrub start, applies the configured step, snaps to step increments, fixes decimal precision, clamps the result, and calls onChange. It is scoped to TweakNumber's scrub interaction.

symbols.does: Ends numeric scrubbing and removes pointer listeners.
symbols.description: This pointerup handler unregisters the window pointermove and pointerup listeners added by onScrubStart. It performs cleanup for the TweakNumber scrub interaction.

symbols.does: Estimates whether a hex color is light enough to need a dark checkmark.
symbols.description: The function accepts #rgb or #rrggbb-style input, expands shorthand hex, parses RGB channels, and applies a weighted luminance threshold. Invalid or unsupported color strings fall through as light.

symbols.does: Renders the selected-color checkmark SVG with contrast-aware stroke color.
symbols.description: The component draws a check path in an SVG and chooses a dark or white stroke based on the light prop. It is used by TweakColor to mark the active swatch or palette.

symbols.does: Renders a curated color or palette picker, with a native color input fallback.
symbols.description: When no options are provided, the component renders input type color. With options, it renders palette chips as radio buttons, supports single colors or arrays of colors, compares selected values through JSON string keys, and calls onChange with the original option shape.

symbols.does: Creates a lowercase JSON-string key for color option comparison.
symbols.description: The helper stringifies an option and lowercases the result so color and palette selections can be compared case-insensitively. String wrapping protects against JSON.stringify returning undefined.

symbols.does: Renders a styled tweak-panel button with optional secondary styling.
symbols.description: The component outputs a button using either the primary .twk-btn class or the secondary variant. It passes clicks through to the provided onClick handler.
